result:
.config restored to its original content and the build is green.

**Summary of changes:**
- `src/drivers/driver.h`: replaced `int qual; int noise;` in `struct wpa_scan_res` with the embedded `struct { int qual; int noise; } sig;`, updated the doc comment.
- Updated every real usage site of `struct wpa_scan_res`'s `qual`/`noise` fields to `sig.qual`/`sig.noise`: `wpa_supplicant/scan.c` (comparator functions, `scan_snr`, `dump_scan_res`), `wpa_supplicant/bss.c` (`wpa_bss_copy_res`, only the `src` side since `dst` is the unrelated `struct wpa_bss`), `wpa_supplicant/events.c`, `src/drivers/driver_bsd.c`, `src/drivers/driver_wext.c`, `src/drivers/driver_nl80211_scan.c`.
- Left untouched all unrelated `qual`/`noise` fields that just happen to share the name: `struct wpa_bss` (`bss.h`, used in `ctrl_iface.c`/`dbus_old_handlers.c`), and `struct iw_quality` (`iwe->u.qual.*`, `stats.qual.*` in `driver_wext.c`).

`make -j8` in `wpa_supplicant/` under the original `.config` builds `wpa_supplicant`, `wpa_cli`, and `wpa_passphrase` with no errors (one pre-existing unrelated warning in `wpa_cli.c`). Note: `driver_wext.c`, `driver_bsd.c`, and `driver_nl80211*.c` aren't part of this sandbox's default build (they need Linux/BSD kernel headers not present on this macOS host) — I verified those edits by reading each call site's pointer type rather than by compiling them.
